content.text:
Generate a file named AGENTS.md that serves as a contributor guide for this repository.
Your goal is to produce a clear, concise, and well-structured document with descriptive headings and actionable explanations for each section.
Follow the outline below, but adapt as needed — add sections if relevant, and omit those that do not apply to this project.

Document Requirements

- Title the document "Repository Guidelines".
- Use Markdown headings (#, ##, etc.) for structure.
- Keep the document concise. 200-400 words is optimal.
- Keep explanations short, direct, and specific to this repository.
- Provide examples where helpful (commands, directory paths, naming patterns).
- Maintain a professional, instructional tone.

Recommended Sections

Project Structure & Module Organization

- Outline the project structure, including where the source code, tests, and assets are located.

Build, Test, and Development Commands

- List key commands for building, testing, and running locally (e.g., npm test, make build).
- Briefly explain what each command does.

Coding Style & Naming Conventions

- Specify indentation rules, language-specific style preferences, and naming patterns.
- Include any formatting or linting tools used.

Testing Guidelines

- Identify testing frameworks and coverage requirements.
- State test naming conventions and how to run tests.

Commit & Pull Request Guidelines

- Summarize commit message conventions found in the project’s Git history.
- Outline pull request requirements (descriptions, linked issues, screenshots, etc.).

(Optional) Add other sections if relevant, such as Security & Configuration Tips, Architecture Overview, or Agent-Specific Instructions.

{
  "name": "vip",
  "private": true,
  "type": "module",
  "engines": {
    "node": ">=23.11.0",
    "npm": ">=10.9.2"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "prepare": "husky",
    "build": "react-router build",
    "dev": "react-router dev",
    "staging": "react-router dev --mode staging",
    "start": "react-router-serve ./build/server/index.js",
    "start:docker": "docker build -t vip . && docker run -p 3000:3000 --env-file .env.production --rm vip",
    "format": "biome format --write .",
    "lint": "biome lint --write .",
    "typecheck": "react-router typegen && tsc",
    "test": "playwright test",
    "generate:anedot": "rm -rf generated-anedot && openapi --input openapi.yaml --output ./generated-anedot --useUnionTypes",
    "generate:subfi": "rm -rf /generated-subfi && cd ../openapi && git pull && npm run generate && cp -r generated ../subfi-pay-dashboard"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@radix-ui/react-accordion": "^1.2.8",

# Welcome to React Router!

A modern, production-ready template for building full-stack React applications using React Router.

[![Open in StackBlitz](https://developer.stackblitz.com/img/open_in_stackblitz.svg)](https://stackblitz.com/github/remix-run/react-router-templates/tree/main/default)

## Features

- 🚀 Server-side rendering
- ⚡️ Hot Module Replacement (HMR)
- 📦 Asset bundling and optimization
- 🔄 Data loading and mutations
- 🔒 TypeScript by default
- 🎉 TailwindCSS for styling
- 📖 [React Router docs](https://reactrouter.com/)

## Getting Started

### Installation

Install the dependencies:

```bash
npm install
```

### Development

Start the development server with HMR:

```bash
npm run dev
```

Your application will be available at `http://localhost:5173`.

## Building for Production

Create a production build:

```bash
npm run build
```

## Deployment

### Docker Deployment

To build and run using Docker:

```bash
docker build -t my-app .

# Run the container
docker run -p 3000:3000 my-app
```

The containerized application can be deployed to any platform that supports Docker, including:

- AWS ECS
- Google Cloud Run
- Azure Container Apps
- Digital Ocean App Platform
- Fly.io
- Railway

### DIY Deployment

If you're familiar with deploying Node applications, the built-in app server is production-ready.

Make sure to deploy the output of `npm run build`

```
├── package.json
├── package-lock.json (or pnpm-lock.yaml, or bun.lockb)
├── build/
│   ├── client/    # Static assets
│   └── server/    # Server-side code
```

## Styling

This template comes with [Tailwind CSS](https://tailwindcss.com/) already configured for a simple default starting experience. You can use whatever CSS framework you prefer.

---

Built with ❤️ using React Router.

**Planning repository guidelines structure**

I'm outlining repository guidelines with six concise sections covering project structure, commands, coding style, testing, commits, and API/config notes, aiming for about 260 words total. **Planning repository guidelines structure**

I'm outlining repository guidelines with six concise sections covering project structure, commands, coding style, testing, commits, and API/config notes, aiming for about 260 words total. payload.name: apply_patch
payload.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: AGENTS.md
+# Repository Guidelines
+
+## Project Structure & Module Organization
+Application code lives in `app/`, with route modules under `app/routes/`, shared UI in `app/components/`, and reusable logic in `app/hooks/` and `lib/`. Static assets (icons, favicons, fonts) sit in `public/`. Generated API clients are checked into `generated-anedot/` and `generated-subfi/`; regenerate them from `openapi.yaml` whenever upstream schemas change. Keep environment-specific config in `.env.*` files referenced by Vite and `start:docker`.
+
+## Build, Test, and Development Commands
+- `npm run dev`: launches the React Router dev server with HMR on port 5173.
+- `npm run staging`: starts the dev server in staging mode for environment flag checks.
+- `npm run build`: produces the SSR-ready bundle in `build/`.
+- `npm run start`: serves the built app via `react-router-serve`.
+- `npm run format` / `npm run lint`: applies Biome formatting and lint fixes.
+- `npm run typecheck`: runs route type generation plus `tsc`.
+- `npm run test`: executes the Playwright end-to-end suite.
+
+## Coding Style & Naming Conventions
+Use TypeScript across new modules. Follow Biome’s defaults (2-space indentation, double quotes) and run format/lint before pushing. Module aliases use the `~` prefix (via `vite-tsconfig-paths`); prefer `~/components/...` imports over relative chains. Name React components in PascalCase, hooks in camelCase starting with `use`, and route files with the React Router file-convention (e.g., `app/routes/dashboard._index.tsx`).
+
+## Testing Guidelines
+Playwright drives the primary regression suite; place specs under `tests/` mirroring the user journeys (`tests/payments/checkout.spec.ts`). Keep scenarios atomic and reset state via API fixtures or MSW where possible. New features must include at least one happy-path E2E check. If you add route loaders or utilities, unit-test pure helpers in `lib/__tests__/` using Vitest (add the harness alongside the file) and invoke `npm run test` locally before review.
+
+## Commit & Pull Request Guidelines
+Commit messages are short, present-tense imperatives (`Fix payout modal`, `Add address validation`). Group related changes and avoid WIP commits. PRs should include a concise summary, linked Linear/Jira issue, test evidence (`npm run test` output, screenshots for UI changes), and call out any migrations or manual follow-up steps. Ensure generated clients are up to date when API contracts change.
*** End Patch

payload.text:
**Revising testing instructions for accuracy**

I'm examining the current testing guidelines that mention Playwright for regression tests and Vitest for unit tests, but Vitest isn’t actually present in the codebase. I’m planning to suggest removing Vitest references and instead recommending Playwright’s test runner for both end-to-end and API tests, organizing tests under `tests/` with clear folders for API and unit tests, to ensure the instructions match the actual dependencies and setup.

I’m planning to suggest removing Vitest references and instead recommending Playwright’s test runner for both end-to-end and API tests, organizing tests under `tests/` with clear folders for API and unit tests, to ensure the instructions match the actual dependencies and setup.

payload.name: apply_patch
payload.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: AGENTS.md
@@
-Playwright drives the primary regression suite; place specs under `tests/` mirroring the user journeys (`tests/payments/checkout.spec.ts`). Keep scenarios atomic and reset state via API fixtures or MSW where possible. New features must include at least one happy-path E2E check. If you add route loaders or utilities, unit-test pure helpers in `lib/__tests__/` using Vitest (add the harness alongside the file) and invoke `npm run test` locally before review.
+Playwright drives the primary regression suite; place specs under `tests/` mirroring the user journeys (`tests/payments/checkout.spec.ts`). Keep scenarios atomic and reset state via API fixtures or MSW where possible. New features must include at least one happy-path E2E check. For lower-level logic, add lightweight specs under `tests/unit/` and run them with the same `npm run test` runner (Playwright supports non-browser API tests) so reviews always see green output.
*** End Patch

payload.message:
Created `AGENTS.md:1` outlining contributor guidance tailored to this React Router + Vite project. Key points:
- Documented the folder layout, including `app/` route modules, shared utilities in `lib/`, static assets in `public/`, and regenerated clients in `generated-anedot/` and `generated-subfi/`.
- Listed everyday commands for dev, builds, linting, formatting, type checks, Playwright tests, and the staging profile.
- Clarified formatting expectations (Biome defaults, TypeScript-first, `~` alias usage) and naming patterns for components, hooks, and route files.
- Described testing expectations around Playwright E2E/API specs, scenario isolation, and keeping at least one happy-path test per feature.
- Summarized commit and PR etiquette: short imperative messages, linked work items, test evidence, and reminders to refresh generated clients when APIs shift.

Next step: share the new guide with the team so contributors know where to find it and can suggest any repo-specific refinements.

content.text:
Created `AGENTS.md:1` outlining contributor guidance tailored to this React Router + Vite project. Key points:
- Documented the folder layout, including `app/` route modules, shared utilities in `lib/`, static assets in `public/`, and regenerated clients in `generated-anedot/` and `generated-subfi/`.
- Listed everyday commands for dev, builds, linting, formatting, type checks, Playwright tests, and the staging profile.
- Clarified formatting expectations (Biome defaults, TypeScript-first, `~` alias usage) and naming patterns for components, hooks, and route files.
- Described testing expectations around Playwright E2E/API specs, scenario isolation, and keeping at least one happy-path test per feature.
- Summarized commit and PR etiquette: short imperative messages, linked work items, test evidence, and reminders to refresh generated clients when APIs shift.

Next step: share the new guide with the team so contributors know where to find it and can suggest any repo-specific refinements.
